evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars for 4 classes across 5 conditions with 0–100% Y-axis fully convey the brief's proportional composition claim; axes and legend are clearly labelled.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=none set; PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type3 bitmap glyphs (confirmed via 'strings figure.pdf'), not editable TrueType/Type1; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Seaborn 'deep' hex colors used (#4C72B0 blue, #55A868 green, #C44E52 red, #CCB974 yellow); green and red co-appear as categorical classes (not directional), risking confusion for deuteranopes; no explicit CB-safe palette (e.g. Wong/Tol) chosen.
evidence.layout_density: Legend placed outside the axes with bbox_to_anchor=(1.01,1) and tight_layout; bars fill the panel well with no gaping whitespace, no label overlaps, Y-range exactly 0–1.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption is purely descriptive ('proportions are taken directly from data.json') with no specific numerical claim or finding; a reviewer would request a key result be stated.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is 12 KB and contains Type3 font objects (DejaVuSans embedded as bitmap glyphs); text is not selectable/editable because pdf.fonttype=42 was not set.
evidence.file_format: All four required files (figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial; figure.svg also provided as a bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies the chart type and variables but states no specific finding or numerical result (e.g. 'Class A dominates condition X at ~50%'); purely procedural description.

overall_strengths: Clean, uncluttered layout with well-sized text, no overlapping labels, and a correctly bounded 0–1 percent Y-axis that fully satisfies the brief.
All required output files are present and correctly formatted; the generation script is self-contained and reproducible.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=none) causes fonts to be embedded as non-editable Type3 bitmap glyphs in the PDF, failing vector-text requirements.
Caption provides no specific numerical finding or scientific claim, and the palette uses red+green as categorical classes without verifying colorblind safety against a standard CB-safe set.
